During the middle ages. by the century, four classes were

recognised: (1) Clergy, (2) SOldiers-Hereditary nobles who felt it was
their business to fight and govern, (3) Townsmen-Those engaged in
trade and industry, and (4) Peasants-serfs and others. Thus we see a
resemblance to the four Hindu castes: (1) K.shatriyas-Sol<fiers, executives,
~aders, administrative and civic offi<ers. (3) Vaisyas-merchants, ranchmen,

In early America, there were only three professions-law. theology
and medicine. Today we have engineering, advertising. pharmacy, and
many others. In this age of specialization. one is seldom simply a lawyer:
he is a patent lawyer, criminal lawyer. civil lawyer. anti-trvst lawyer. etc.
